description: Procedural Sound System — fully synthesized audio via Web Audio API with engine drone, SFX, adaptive music, and mute toggle. Zero audio files.

userStories.title: Audio engine foundation and AudioManager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a centralized AudioManager using the Web Audio API so that all sound systems share one AudioContext and can be globally controlled.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an AudioManager object at module scope in src/main.js with a single AudioContext
AudioContext is created/resumed on first user interaction (Enter key press on title screen) to comply with browser autoplay policy
Expose a masterGain node connected to AudioContext.destination for global volume control
Expose a muted boolean state and toggleMute() function that sets masterGain.gain to 0 or 1
Provide helper: playTone(freq, duration, type, gainValue) — creates oscillator+gain, connects through masterGain, auto-disconnects on end
Provide helper: createNoise(duration, filterFreq, filterType) — generates white noise buffer, applies filter, connects through masterGain, auto-disconnects
All helpers use gain envelopes (short attack/release of ~0.01s) to avoid clicks and pops
Helpers use oscillator.onended to disconnect nodes and prevent memory leaks
No performance degradation — game maintains 60 FPS with AudioManager initialized
Verify in browser that AudioContext initializes on first Enter press (check console or state)

userStories.title: Engine drone sound tied to scroll speed
userStories.description: As a player, I want to hear a continuous engine hum while driving so that speed is perceptible by sound.
acceptanceCriteria: Engine sound uses a sawtooth oscillator with a low-pass filter (cutoff ~400-800Hz)
Engine pitch maps proportionally to scrollSpeed: ~60Hz at 200px/s → ~180Hz at 800px/s
Engine volume ramps up smoothly with speed using setTargetAtTime (not abrupt changes)
Engine oscillator starts when entering playingState and stops when leaving playingState
Use long-lived oscillator nodes with parameter automation (frequency.setTargetAtTime) — do NOT recreate nodes per frame
No clicks or pops on start, stop, or parameter changes (use gain ramp-up/down of ~0.05s)
Verify in browser that engine pitch audibly rises as the car accelerates

userStories.title: Road ambience and nitro boost audio
userStories.description: As a player, I want road noise and a nitro boost whoosh to add depth to the driving experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Road ambience uses a white noise buffer through a band-pass filter (~300-800Hz center)
Road noise volume and filter bandwidth increase with scrollSpeed
Road noise starts/stops together with engine (playingState enter/exit)
Nitro boost temporarily raises engine pitch by ~30% and adds a high-freq sawtooth harmonic (~2x engine freq) with its own gain node
Nitro audio effect activates when nitroTimer > 0 and deactivates when nitro ends
All parameter transitions use setTargetAtTime for smooth crossfades
Verify in browser that road noise is audible and nitro pickup changes the sound

userStories.title: Collision and explosion SFX
userStories.description: As a player, I want impactful sounds on collision and explosion so that danger feels real and dramatic.
acceptanceCriteria: Fatal collision triggers a metallic crash: short noise burst (high-pass ~2kHz, 0.15s) + low-freq impact thump (sine ~60Hz, 0.15s decay)
Explosion sequence (explosionState) plays a layered boom: low sine sweep 80→30Hz over 0.5s + noise burst with decaying low-pass filter + crackle (3-5 random short noise bursts spaced over 1.5s)
Shield break plays a crystalline shatter: high-freq sine sweep 2kHz→500Hz over 0.2s + short noise burst
Explosion is the loudest event in the game (gain ~0.8 vs ~0.3-0.5 for other SFX)
No audio clipping — use appropriate gain values
Collision SFX triggers from handleVehicleCollision() and explosionState.onEnter()
Shield break SFX triggers when shield absorbs a hit (existing shieldBreakFlash logic)
Verify in browser that collision and explosion sounds are distinct and impactful

userStories.title: Collectible pickup SFX
userStories.description: As a player, I want satisfying audio feedback when collecting items so that pickups feel rewarding.
acceptanceCriteria: Fuel pickup: warm ascending sine tone 400→800Hz over 0.15s with soft envelope
Coin pickup: bright metallic ding (triangle wave ~1200Hz, 0.1s, sharp attack). When collecting a cluster, each coin dings at slightly higher pitch (+50Hz per coin in sequence)
Nitro pickup: aggressive sawtooth sweep 200→600Hz over 0.3s
Shield pickup: ethereal tone using two detuned sines (~500Hz and ~505Hz creating beat frequency, 0.4s with slow fade-in)
Each SFX triggers at the point in code where the item is collected (fuel in updateFuelItems, coins in updateCoins, nitro in updateNitroItems, shield in updateShieldItems)
Each sound is distinct and instantly recognizable
Verify in browser that each pickup type has a different, satisfying sound

userStories.title: Near miss, combo, and bonus SFX
userStories.description: As a player, I want audio feedback on near misses and bonuses to feel the adrenaline of risky driving.
acceptanceCriteria: Near miss plays a quick whoosh: band-passed noise burst 0.1s (~1-3kHz)
Combo multiplier increase (×2, ×3, etc.) adds an ascending pitch stinger: sine tone at 600Hz + combo×100Hz, 0.15s
Survivor bonus (30s clean) plays a triumphant short major chord: three sines at 400/500/600Hz, 0.3s with gentle decay
Overtake bonus plays a subtle low filtered sawtooth vroom, 0.15s
Near miss SFX triggers where nearMissFlash is set in the existing near miss detection code
Survivor bonus SFX triggers where SURVIVOR_BONUS points are added
Verify in browser that near miss whoosh is audible when passing close to traffic

userStories.title: Fuel warning beep
userStories.description: As a player, I want an audio warning when fuel is critically low so I don't run out unexpectedly.
acceptanceCriteria: When fuel < 25%, play a repeating warning beep: square wave ~800Hz, 0.08s on, 0.4s off
When fuel < 15%, beep faster: 0.08s on, 0.25s off
When fuel < 5%, beep rapidly: 0.08s on, 0.12s off
Warning stops immediately when fuel is collected above 25%
Warning respects mute state (goes through masterGain)
Implement using a timer tracking next beep time, checked in the fuel update logic
Verify in browser that beep starts at 25% fuel and accelerates as fuel drops

userStories.title: Title screen ambient drone
userStories.description: As a player, I want ambient sound on the title screen so the game feels atmospheric from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: Title screen plays a low sustained sine drone (~50-60Hz) with slow LFO amplitude modulation (~0.3Hz)
Add a soft filtered noise pad (low-pass ~200Hz, very low gain) for texture
Drone starts when titleState is entered (after AudioContext is resumed)
Drone crossfades out over 0.3s when transitioning to playingState
On return to title (after game over → retry is NOT title, so this is only initial load), drone restarts
Verify in browser that title screen has a calm, mysterious ambient sound

userStories.title: Playing state rhythmic beat
userStories.description: As a player, I want a rhythmic beat during gameplay that intensifies with speed for a cinematic driving experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Synthesized kick drum: sine 150→50Hz with 0.1s exponential decay, triggered on beat
Hi-hat on off-beats: short noise burst with high-pass 8kHz, 0.05s decay
Optional sub bass note on every 4th beat: sine 40-50Hz, 0.2s
Tempo (BPM) maps to scrollSpeed: ~90 BPM at 200px/s → ~150 BPM at 800px/s
Beat scheduling uses AudioContext.currentTime for precise timing (not setTimeout/setInterval)
Implement a beat scheduler that pre-schedules ~2-4 beats ahead and refills as time advances
Beat starts when entering playingState; scheduler stops when leaving playingState
Tempo changes smoothly as speed increases (recalculate interval on each scheduler tick)
Verify in browser that beat is audible and tempo increases with speed

userStories.title: Explosion silence and game over somber drone
userStories.description: As a player, I want the music to cut on explosion and shift to a somber tone on game over for cinematic impact.
acceptanceCriteria: On transition to explosionState: engine, road noise, and beat stop abruptly (gain → 0 in 0.05s, then disconnect)
During explosionState: only explosion SFX plays, plus a low ominous rumble (filtered noise, very low freq ~40-80Hz, slow fade-in over 1s)
On transition to gameOverState: rumble crossfades into a slow somber drone — two detuned low sines (~48Hz and ~51Hz) creating dissonance + very soft filtered noise tail
Game over drone loops until player presses Enter (retry)
On retry (transition back to playingState via titleState path), all game over audio stops cleanly and engine/beat restart
No orphaned audio nodes after state transitions (all nodes properly stopped and disconnected)
Verify in browser that explosion cuts the music and game over has a somber atmosphere

userStories.title: Mute/unmute HUD button and M key toggle
userStories.description: As a player, I want a visible mute button so I can toggle sound on/off without leaving the game.
acceptanceCriteria: Speaker icon rendered in top-right corner of canvas using simple canvas drawing (lines/arcs, no external assets)
Icon shows speaker-on (speaker body + 2 sound wave arcs) when unmuted
Icon shows speaker-off (speaker body + X mark, no wave arcs) when muted
Clicking the icon area (add click event listener on canvas, check coordinates) toggles AudioManager.toggleMute()
Pressing M key also toggles mute (using existing consumeKey pattern)
Icon is rendered in all game states (title, playing, explosion, game over)
Icon is semi-transparent (~0.5 alpha) to not distract from gameplay, full opacity on hover would be nice but not required
Verify in browser that icon is visible, clickable, and mute/unmute works
